title: I Have Abundant Confidence!
translatorNote: This translation is intended to carry Kalaignar's voice into clear, contemporary English rather than recast the letter as literary or academic prose. It preserves the source's argument, political directness, rhetorical questions, repetition, irony, factual detail, and paragraph order. Names, dates, figures, quotations, and intentional English expressions are retained. Where Tamil idiom cannot be reproduced literally without sounding unnatural, the English follows its sense and rhetorical force without adding claims absent from the source. The original Tamil is reproduced in full below the translation and remains the authoritative text. `Udanpirappē` is retained in Tamil transliteration rather than flattened into “brother,” “sister,” or “comrade.” Literally evoking “one born alongside me,” Kalaignar uses it as a distinctive address of shared identity, equality, affection, and solidarity within the movement. The source scan confirms the closing English phrase as “Public Memory is short” and the letter date as 19.3.2016; both are damaged or absent in the OCR-derived chapter text. The Tamil reading copy below repairs only source-confirmed OCR defects. The canonical Tamil source remains unchanged.
paragraphs: What exactly is happening in the Tamil Nadu Government led by Jayalalithaa? Over the past five years, various allegations have periodically emerged against all the ministers in office. In particular, corruption allegations involving several crores of rupees were made against the Electricity Minister, supported by preliminary evidence. On every such occasion, Chief Minister Jayalalithaa ignored them and remained indifferent. What is the position now?

What kind of news do we find whenever we open the daily newspapers? O. Panneerselvam occupies the position next to Jayalalithaa in the Cabinet. Indeed, when court proceedings twice forced Jayalalithaa to step down, it was he who served as Chief Minister. Even today, he is the Finance Minister. But what has become of him? It is said that Jayalalithaa orally instructed the intelligence department to monitor his activities, and that its officers have collected details of the property Panneerselvam owns in various places, the irregularities he has committed, and the crores collected by his supporters on the promise of securing MLA seats.

When the opposition parties raised the death of Nagamuthu, a priest from a Scheduled Caste community who took his own life after leaving a letter because of the wrongdoing of O. Panneerselvam's younger brother, was any action taken against the minister? None at all. Today, it is said, the intelligence department is investigating the wrongdoings of the same OPS's sons and submitting reports.

Every day, reports appear that three ministers—O. Panneerselvam, Natham Viswanathan and Palaniappan—collected crores upon crores from people across the State by promising to secure MLA seats for them. It is said that the “Garden” has ordered these three ministers not to leave Chennai. They have also reportedly been forbidden from participating in party functions.

When the opposition parties spent the past five years repeatedly laying out allegations against these very ministers and their supporters, did the Chief Minister pay any attention? Now that all those allegations have turned out to be true, what explanation does this Government offer the people? Are the actions now being taken openly merely another of Jayalalithaa's customary fraudulent dramas? Are they deceitful performances staged to fool the people and divert their attention?

But the people of this country are not fools who can be deceived by Jayalalithaa's dramas! They have already watched similar dramas staged by her in the past and understood the stories behind them. Why, what did Jayalalithaa herself say about Sasikala, who now lives with her? How did she behave? Does the country not know? Is it not clear that Sasikala's expulsion and subsequent readmission were merely scenes in a drama?

The posts held by several supporters of Finance Minister O. Panneerselvam have been taken away: AIADMK Fishermen's Wing Deputy Secretary T. Ramesh; Velachery MLA Ashok; Ellapatti Murugan of Theni district; Kanchipuram district secretaries Chinnaiya and Thandarai Manoharan; Vijayabaskar; Tambaram Karikalan; and Varagur Arunachalam. K. Mariappan, the Palani town secretary and a close friend of Natham Viswanathan, was also removed from his post. Subsequently, M.M. Babu was removed as South Chennai South district AIADMK secretary. T.R.N. Varadarajan was stripped of his responsibility as Jayalalithaa Peravai secretary; R.R. Jegadees was removed as Theni district students' wing secretary; and M. Rajkumar was removed as the Andipatti union district representative.

Three people were arrested in Chennai: Seeni Kandasamy, who had been described as O. Panneerselvam's shadow; Ramesh Kumar, a former Poes Garden employee; and Sivakumar of Sivakasi. During questioning, they reportedly said that they had rented a luxury bungalow in Neelankarai and, with the help of Panneerselvam and district secretaries, collected crores from numerous people across the State. All three are said to have declared: “We are innocent. We were paid only salaries. We handed over all the money to the leadership. We collected money from a great many people and passed it on.” It is further said that O. Panneerselvam has sidelined them and confined them to Chennai. Even though the weddings in the families of his close friend Chellamuthu and Kadamalai-Mayilai Union Chairman Murukkodai Ramar were to be held under his leadership, he reportedly did not attend. Jayalalithaa's photograph, which would ordinarily appear on those invitations, was apparently missing. It is said that he began the journey to attend the wedding, only for it to be cancelled.
One newspaper published a report under the very headline, “Where is Minister OPS?” The report said:

> “Following the difficulty that Tamil Nadu Finance Minister Panneerselvam has encountered with the ruling leadership, rumours concerning him are flying in every direction. In particular, reports about him are appearing on WhatsApp every second, causing anxiety among his supporters. Rumours are now being circulated through WhatsApp that Minister O. Panneerselvam has been confined to the ministerial bungalow in Chennai; that he has been confined to a bungalow on Chennai's East Coast Road; and that he has been confined to a bungalow in a grove belonging to him in Theni district.”
That is what *Dinamalar* wrote today.

The posts of several supporters of Natham Viswanathan were subsequently taken away: Dindigul Information Technology Wing Secretary Dharmalingam; Sanarpatti Union Jayalalithaa Peravai Joint Secretary Mani alias Subramani; and Mettukadai Selvaraj, a general-council member from the Natham Assembly constituency. Among them, Mettukadai Selvaraj is a close friend of Kannan, Natham Viswanathan's son-in-law and district functionary. He is described as Kannan's benami.

In Thoothukudi, police have arrested Krishnamurthy, an assistant to Minister Shanmuganathan. The action followed a complaint that Krishnamurthy collected money and operated as an intermediary for matters ranging from party posts to employment and transfers in Thoothukudi district. In Tirunelveli district, Palayamkottai police have registered a case against government contractor R.S. Murugan, who allegedly acted as OPS's benami. He is absconding.

What is the Government's explanation for these reports? When the opposition parties spent the past five years repeatedly laying out allegations against these very ministers and their supporters, did the Chief Minister pay any attention? Now that all those allegations have turned out to be true, what explanation does this Government offer the people? Are the actions now being taken openly merely another of Jayalalithaa's customary fraudulent dramas? Are they deceitful performances staged to fool the people and divert their attention?

But the people of this country are not fools who can be deceived by Jayalalithaa's dramas! They have already watched similar dramas staged by her in the past and understood the stories behind them. Why, what did Jayalalithaa herself say about Sasikala, who now lives with her? How did she behave? Does the country not know? Is it not clear that Sasikala's expulsion and subsequent readmission were merely scenes in a drama?
In a statement issued on 27 August 1996, Jayalalithaa said:

> “Today, I wish to state my position openly and clearly regarding the criticism directed against me over the past few months by some party members, the ridicule in the press and adverse political criticism, all centred on my association with one family. Among the reasons I identified in my address to the party executive committee as having caused our defeat, the principal reason cited unanimously by everyone was the association that existed between Sasikala and her family and me. It is plainly evident that an impression has taken root in the public mind that Sasikala and her family operated as a centre of power and as an authority beyond the law and rules. The newspapers have written extensively about Sasikala and her family. Many things have been said about this family from public platforms and at street-corner meetings. Some of these are true incidents and reports. Because Sasikala stayed in my own house, the objectionable activities of some of her relatives created a deep but mistaken impression among the people that they had taken place with my permission and support. Therefore, as the party's Udanpirappugal insist that I must decide whether the party is greater or my association with Sasikala is greater, I inform everyone that, in accordance with everyone's wishes, from this day forward I sever every association I had with Sasikala and her family. I state categorically to everyone that from this day forward I have no foster son. In keeping with the unanimous request of the party's Udanpirappugal, the compulsion and necessity have arisen for me to separate myself completely from Sasikala and Sudhakaran.”
Jayalalithaa thus played the part of someone who had made a decision and set out to cleanse both her party and her home! Have the people forgotten?

In a government, the Cabinet bears collective responsibility. When ministers commit wrongdoing, it is the Chief Minister who must accept responsibility for it. How many times were ministers changed during these five years of AIADMK rule? If ministers were changed, it was because they had committed wrongs and irregularities. Some of those removed ministers were later inducted into the Cabinet again. If so, what became of the wrongs they had committed earlier?

How long did the letter written like a dramatic monologue survive? For how many days did Jayalalithaa stand firm by what she had written in it? Like the saying, “The same old blind woman again—open the door,” Sasikala's family once more joined Jayalalithaa and began living in the same house. Did even that one-act drama last? A few months later came a startling turn in the drama: a statement announcing that Sasikala and her family had been expelled from the party. Her husband was arrested, as were close relatives. Reports appeared continuously in the newspapers. Later, Jayalalithaa took Sasikala back. Her husband emerged from prison and told journalists, seething with anger: “I was arrested on a false complaint. Those who filed the complaint have now withdrawn it. The police tried to kill me in an ‘encounter.’” Later, his anger too subsided and dissolved, and that case simply lost all force.
All these are incidents to which Jayalalithaa has grown accustomed, because every one of them was a deliberately staged scene! Are similar events now being staged again with the election approaching?

There are many, many more spectacles, strange turns and deceptive dramas yet to come! It is your duty to take the coming scenes—and their underlying meaning—to the public and explain them clearly. I have abundant confidence that you will perform that duty with precision.

In a government, the Cabinet bears collective responsibility. When ministers commit wrongdoing, it is the Chief Minister who must accept responsibility for it. How many times were ministers changed during these five years of AIADMK rule? If ministers were changed, it was because they had committed wrongs and irregularities. Some of those removed ministers were later inducted into the Cabinet again. If so, what became of the wrongs they had committed? Has it not been the continuing practice to remove ministers who committed irregularities and then, after the public has forgotten those irregularities, take them back again? How many different ways do they exploit the fact that “Public Memory is short”? If so many ministers in a single Cabinet have committed wrongdoing, what does that say about the functioning of the party? Can such a party be allowed to return to power? It is not surprising for opposition parties to make allegations against ministers in a government. But when those in power themselves level one allegation after another against senior members of their own Cabinet, is any further explanation of that government even necessary?

Udanpirappē, is it not your duty to explain these continuing dramas and all these spectacles and strange events to the public and remind them? I know very well that you will already be explaining all this to the people of the country in a way that helps them understand it and retain that understanding in their memory. Even so, is it not my task to share the information with you? It is only in that spirit that I bring all this to your attention.

There are many, many more spectacles, strange turns and deceptive dramas yet to come! It is your duty to take the coming scenes—and their underlying meaning—to the public and explain them clearly. I have abundant confidence that you will perform that duty with precision.
With affection,
M.K.
